idea-plugin.name: JSONL Viewer
idea-plugin.description:
A fast viewer for JSON Lines files (.jsonl / .ndjson, plain or .gz), built for files that are far too large for the normal editor.

Opening a .jsonl (or .jsonl.gz) file adds a Records tab that shows the file as a virtualized, scrollable list of one-line record previews. Selecting a record pretty-prints it below with JSON syntax highlighting and independently foldable nested objects and arrays.

  • Handles huge files — the file is streamed and indexed by record offset; it is never loaded into the editor's document or PSI, so multi-hundred-megabyte files open instantly without hitting the IDE's size limits.
  • On-demand pretty-printing — each record is formatted only when selected; nested structures fold independently.
  • Full-text search — search across the entire file; the list filters to the matching records with the match highlighted.
  • Read-only — never modifies your files; the raw text stays available on a second tab.
  • Reads gzip files — .jsonl.gz / .ndjson.gz are decompressed to a temp file on open and viewed with the same streaming Records tab; the temp file is removed when the editor closes.

Works in any IntelliJ-based IDE (IntelliJ IDEA, Rider, PyCharm, and others).
idea-plugin.change-notes:
• 1.1.0 — Open gzip-compressed JSON Lines files (.jsonl.gz / .ndjson.gz / .jsonlines.gz / .ldjson.gz).
  • 1.0.0 — Initial release: streaming Records viewer for .jsonl / .ndjson with a virtualized record list, on-demand pretty-printing with nested folding, and full-text search.

action.description: Expand all nested objects and arrays in this record
Collapse all nested objects and arrays in this record
